citations.description: Ticket 49ae-258b-4534-4d83, comment by Joe Oakhart: 'Not self-attesting an operator soak sign-off; closing as superseded.'

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] a soak period with the floors default-ON has elapsed with no must-suppress (gold-set) regression reported; the attestation names the soak window and evidence.
findings.detail: No concrete operator attestation naming a soak window and evidence appears in the ticket. The only comment (from Joe Oakhart, the ticket author) explicitly states 'Not self-attesting an operator soak sign-off; closing as superseded.' This is a refusal to attest, not an attestation. The operator-attested bar requires at least one verifiable specific (a reference id/URL, a named actor with measured outcome, or a timestamp/date plus evidence) — none are present.
findings.remediation: Record a ticket comment (or attached artifact) that names: (1) the exact soak window (start date/commit/deploy id and end date), (2) the evidence of no must-suppress gold-set regression during that period (e.g. a link to the monitoring run, log output, or dogfood report), and (3) the operator who authorized the conclusion. The comment on the superseded story 4cdf-e52c-e68e-4f7d could also serve if it contains these specifics.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
